text.title: AWS Lambda Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules every application of `AWS Lambda` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each function's memory MUST be tuned via AWS Lambda Power Tuning state machine; the chosen memory must be justified in the function's README.
rule.rationale: Default 128MB memory is rarely optimal; Power Tuning typically halves cost or latency.

rule.statement: Latency-sensitive Lambdas (p99 < 500ms required) MUST use SnapStart (Java, .NET, Python, Ruby) OR Provisioned Concurrency. On-demand cold starts are forbidden for that tier.

rationale.source: AWS Lambda SnapStart docs; cold-start postmortems

rule.rationale: Cold starts for latency-sensitive endpoints break SLOs; SnapStart / PC closes that gap.

rule.statement: Lambdas triggered by SQS / Kinesis / DynamoDB Streams / EventBridge MUST be idempotent (use Powertools Idempotency or equivalent).

rationale.source: AWS Lambda Powertools docs; event-driven postmortems

rule.rationale: Event sources retry on failure; non-idempotent handlers double-charge / double-write / corrupt state.

rule.statement: Lambda logs MUST be JSON-structured with correlation_id, request_id, and Lambda Powertools Logger or equivalent; print() / unstructured logs are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Unstructured logs break CloudWatch Logs Insights queries + lineage; JSON logs are searchable + parseable.

rule.statement: Production Lambdas MUST have AWS X-Ray active tracing on; trace headers propagated to downstream services (HTTP / SDK calls).
rule.rationale: Without tracing, debugging cross-service latency is guesswork; X-Ray surfaces the actual hop costs.

rule.statement: Async event sources MUST have a DLQ + retry policy bounded (max retries ≤ 3 for SQS, retention 14d on DLQ). Sync Lambdas MUST return structured errors.
rule.rationale: Without DLQ, failed async invocations vanish; bounded retries prevent infinite loops.

rule.statement: Execution roles MUST be per-function (or per-tightly-coupled group), with IAM Access Analyzer policy generation + manual review. Wildcard `*` resources forbidden.

rationale.source: AWS IAM Best Practices; IAM Access Analyzer

rule.rationale: Shared / wildcard execution roles leak across functions; per-function roles bound blast radius.

rule.statement: Lambda layers MUST be pinned by ARN + version (no `$LATEST`); shared layers MUST be cross-account-shared with read-only resource policy.
rule.rationale: `$LATEST` layer pins drift silently and break running functions during AWS infra events; ARN+version is the only reproducible pin.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
